description: Tier-B LLM-judge eval cases (live, non-deterministic). Each case runs a skill/agent prompt against a live Aseprite session, then an LLM judge scores the result against a weighted rubric grounded in rules/. NOT run in CI (needs Aseprite + tokens). CI only structurally validates this file via evals/judge.py (wired into run.py as tier_b_cases_wellformed). Run a case manually: `python evals/judge.py --emit <case_id>` prints the judge prompt; paste the live result + prompt to a judge model and record the score in evals/RESULTS.md.

scoring.scale: Each criterion is scored 0.0-1.0 by the judge. Case score = Σ(criterion_score × weight). A criterion's weights across a case sum to 1.0.
scoring.rule: A case PASSES when case_score >= pass_threshold AND no criterion marked must_pass scores < 0.5.

cases.prompt: Scaffold a new 32x32 goblin sprite with the goblin-default palette and a rigged, animation-ready layer base.

rubric.desc: Called live_preflight and only proceeded when ready:true (never fell back to batch/file tools).
Canvas is exactly 32x32; size is intentional, not arbitrary.
A palette/ramps were locked BEFORE any pixels were drawn; only palette colors used.
Standard rig layers exist and are correctly named (Shadow, Legs, Body, ArmL, ArmR, Head), bottom-to-top order, limbs separable.
Base silhouette reads as a goblin at 100%; proportions plausible.
No orphan/off-palette pixels; scratch/AI-draft layers removed or clearly marked.

cases.prompt: Shade the Body layer of this sprite using the skin ramp, light coming from the upper-left.

rubric.desc: Only colors from the existing skin ramp were introduced; no new/off-ramp colors.
Shadows hue-shift cooler/darker and highlights warmer/lighter, not just value changes on one hue.
Light direction is consistent (upper-left) across the form; shadow falls on the opposite side.
No banding (parallel jagged ramp steps) and no pillow-shading (concentric rings ignoring a light source).
Re-running the skill on an already-shaded layer is idempotent / previewable, not destructive.
Only the Body layer was modified; other rig layers untouched.

cases.prompt: Make a 4-frame walk cycle for this rig.

rubric.desc: Contact and Passing key poses are present (pose-to-pose), 4 frames minimum.
Body bobs vertically (low at Contact, high at Passing) — not flat/skating.
Arms counter-swing the legs; loose parts (sack/ears) lag a frame.
Volume/mass is preserved across frames (no popping); animated per-layer, not redrawn.
A `walk` tag wraps the frames with the correct loop direction.
Per-frame durations are in the ~120-150 ms walk range and set explicitly.

cases.prompt: Export this animated sprite as a spritesheet PNG plus JSON metadata, ready for a game engine.

rubric.desc: Produced the requested spritesheet PNG (and/or GIF) at the correct path; files actually exist.
Emitted JSON metadata containing frame rects, tags, and per-frame durations usable by an engine.
Scratch/AI-draft layers are excluded from the exported output.
Sheet packing is sane (consistent cell size; documented or power-of-two when relevant).
Used live tools/correct working-dir-relative paths; no silent batch fallback for the live source.

cases.prompt: Plan an attack cycle (club swing) for the goblin rig before any frames are drawn.

rubric.desc: Output is a frame-by-frame plan table (frame | pose | moving layers/offsets | ms), not drawn frames — plans in its own context without main-context bloat.
Anticipation (wind-up, held ~300-400 ms) precedes the strike.
Strike is a fast arc (~60-100 ms); weapon may smear but stays a big readable shape, never a stub.
Follow-through/recovery overshoots then settles; loose parts lag.
Plan moves rig layers (limb offsets), not a full redraw.
Flags any rig gaps that would block the planned motion, and names the tag + loop type.

cases.prompt: This goblin is drawn on a single baked layer. Split it into an animatable rig.

rubric.desc: Outputs an ordered layer plan (bottom→top) stating what each layer contains and why.
Correct anatomy assignments: chin→Head, shoulders/neck-base→Body, arms own layers, weapon rides the holding hand.
PascalCase + L/R naming from the character's POV (ArmL/ArmR, LegL/LegR).
Gives a per-layer soloed clean/needs-fix verdict; each layer reads as a complete shape with no seam bleed.
Scratch/AI-draft layers are flagged for removal before export.
For 3/4 view, near/far limb depth & occlusion are noted.
